# WO-002 Research: Add Design Ops Skills

**Date:** 2026-04-13
**Researcher:** Research Agent
**Ticket:** WO-002

---

## Summary

This document captures technical research findings for implementing seven design ops skills in the DesignOps Figma plugin. Each skill maps to a specific designer workflow at Detroit Labs. All seven skills are feasible using a combination of the Figma Plugin API, Figma REST API, and existing open-source tooling. Several skills share infrastructure around the Variables API and token transformation pipelines.

---

## Resolved Blockers

### Blocker 1 — Detroit Labs Canonical Project Structure ✅

DL projects follow a three-folder organization under the team account:

**Folder: `Strategy/`**
| File | Type | Template |
|---|---|---|
| Workshop | FigJam | "Discovery Workshop Template" |
| Summary | Slides | "Discovery Summary Template" |
| Wireframes | Design | (no template) |

**Folder: `Design-Systems/`**
| File | Type | Reference |
|---|---|---|
| Foundations | Design | [foundations-master-kit](https://www.figma.com/design/HrSL0JGanWIAbcJOJAyucz/foundations-master-kit?node-id=33-98) |

**Folder: `Master-Files/`**
| File | Type | Reference |
|---|---|---|
| iOS Masterfile | Design | [Masterfile-template](https://www.figma.com/design/C9C0XpIdj1WS3klOugVzGM/Masterfile-template) |
| Android Masterfile | Design | Same template, Android cover/page variant |
| RIVE Masterfile | Design | Same template, RIVE cover/page variant |

The Masterfile template (file key `C9C0XpIdj1WS3klOugVzGM`) contains covers for iOS, Android, RIVE, and Foundations — `/new-project` should clone the appropriate variant per platform. The Foundations file (file key `HrSL0JGanWIAbcJOJAyucz`) defines the internal page/section structure for the design system file.

**Impact on `/new-project`:** The scaffold JSON config should map directly to this folder hierarchy. File creation via the Figma REST API (`POST /v1/files`) will place files into the correct team/project folder. Template-based creation (Workshop, Summary) can use `POST /v1/files/:file_key/duplicate` to clone from the template file keys.

**Foundations file — confirmed template:** The Foundations file is cloned from the **foundations-Agent-Kit** (`rJQsr4aou5yjzUhaEM0I2f`), NOT the previously referenced foundations-master-kit. After cloning, `/create-design-system` fills the kit's variable slots with the project's brand tokens.

**foundations-Agent-Kit structure (from ToC, node `59:78`):**

| Section | Groups |
|---|---|
| Foundations | Brand Assets (5 items), Style Guide (5 items) |
| Base Components | ~18 component types |
| Composites | Navigation, Messaging, Cards, Tiles, Information Blocks |
| Sections | Sheets |
| Inputs | 7 types |

**Pre-planning required:** Before build, a live Figma session must inspect the kit's variable collections (`get_variable_defs` on file `rJQsr4aou5yjzUhaEM0I2f`) to catalog all variable slots that `/create-design-system` must populate. This is a planning-phase prerequisite, not a research-phase blocker.

---

### Blocker 2 — DL Token Naming Schema ✅

**Decision:** Tailwind-derived primitives as the single source, with platform-scoped collections that reference the same primitive values.

**Recommended architecture:**

```
Collection: Primitives          (raw values — never used directly in designs)
  color/blue/500 = #3B82F6
  color/neutral/900 = #111827
  font-size/base = 16
  ...

Collection: Web (Tailwind)      (semantic aliases → Primitives)
  color/background = {color/neutral/50}
  color/foreground = {color/neutral/900}
  color/primary = {color/blue/500}
  spacing/4 = 16px
  ...

Collection: Android (M3)        (M3 role aliases → Primitives)
  md.sys.color.primary = {color/blue/500}
  md.sys.color.on-primary = #ffffff
  md.sys.typescale.body-large.size = {font-size/base}
  ...

Collection: iOS (HIG)           (HIG semantic aliases → Primitives)
  color/label = {color/neutral/900}
  color/systemBlue = {color/blue/500}
  typography/body = {font-size/base}
  ...
```

**Rationale:** A single primitive collection avoids value duplication. Platform collections use Figma variable aliasing to reference primitives, so updating a primitive value cascades across all platforms. This is the most efficient structure for designers (one source of truth) while maintaining platform-correct naming for handoff. Style Dictionary can transform any collection to its platform output format (CSS variables, Tailwind config, Kotlin, Swift).

**Naming convention:** Primitives follow Tailwind scale naming. M3 collection follows `md.sys.*` and `md.ref.*` namespacing. HIG collection follows Apple's semantic naming (`label`, `systemBlue`, `background`).

---

### Blocker 3 — Figma Plan Tier for Variables REST API ✅

Deferred to team confirmation, but noted as non-blocking for initial implementation. The Plugin API (`figma.variables.*`) is available on all plan tiers. The REST API bulk write (`PUT /v1/files/:file_key/variables`) requires Professional plan or above. `/sync-design-system` companion CLI will require this; the plugin-only skills (`/new-project`, `/create-design-system`) will use the Plugin API path first and the REST path as an enhancement.

---

### Blocker 4 — Localization Platform for `/new-language` ✅

**Decision:** No external translation API or platform integration. The skill will be fully agent-driven using inline text handling:

1. Duplicate the selected frame/page using `node.clone()` + `figma.createPage()`
2. Traverse all text nodes (`figma.currentPage.findAll(n => n.type === 'TEXT')`)
3. Pass the extracted strings to the agent (Claude) for translation in-context
4. Agent writes translated strings back to the cloned text nodes via the Plugin API

This approach requires no API keys, no external services, and no additional permissions — the agent provides the translation capability directly. RTL languages will get a warning banner added to the cloned page noting layout mirroring requirements.

---

## Key Findings

### Skill 1: `/new-project`

**Purpose:** Programmatically scaffold a new Figma project with standard Detroit Labs file structure — foundations, master component files, etc.

**Relevant APIs:**
- `figma.createFrame()` — creates frames/pages for foundations, component pages, etc.
- `figma.createComponent()` — creates master components
- `figma.variables.createVariableCollection()` / `figma.variables.createVariable()` — sets up token/variable scaffolding
- `figma.loadFontAsync()` — loads brand fonts during setup
- Figma REST API `POST /v1/files` — creates new Figma files (requires OAuth scope `file_write`)

**Feasibility:** High. The primary mechanism is `POST /v1/files/:file_key/duplicate` via the Figma REST API — this clones the Agent Kit template into the correct team folder, preserving all pages, components, and variable collection structure. After cloning, `/create-design-system` fills the variable slots with the project's brand tokens.

**Template file keys:**
- Foundations: clone `rJQsr4aou5yjzUhaEM0I2f` (foundations-Agent-Kit)
- Workshop (FigJam): clone "Discovery Workshop Template" key (to be confirmed by designer)
- Summary (Slides): clone "Discovery Summary Template" key (to be confirmed by designer)
- Master Files (iOS/Android/RIVE): clone `C9C0XpIdj1WS3klOugVzGM` (Masterfile-template)

**Open Questions / Risks:**
- ~~What is the canonical Detroit Labs project structure?~~ ✅ Resolved — see Resolved Blockers section
- Should the scaffold also create a GitHub repo or just the Figma file? (Scope decision for planning)
- Workshop and Summary template file keys need to be confirmed by a designer
- Variable slot catalog for the Agent Kit is a required pre-planning step: run `get_variable_defs` on `rJQsr4aou5yjzUhaEM0I2f` in a live Figma session before `/create-design-system` build begins

---

### Skill 2: `/create-design-system [web/android/ios]`

**Purpose:** Pull in platform-appropriate design tokens (Tailwind for web, Material 3 for Android, Apple HIG for iOS), push tokens to Figma canvas as variables and styles, and guide the user interactively through setup if colors/fonts are missing.

**Relevant APIs:**
- `figma.variables.createVariableCollection()` + `figma.variables.createVariable()` — creates token variables in Figma
- `figma.createTextStyle()`, `figma.createPaintStyle()` — creates Figma styles for typography and color
- Plugin UI (HTML panel) — drives interactive setup wizard
- Figma Variables REST API `PUT /v1/files/:file_key/variables` — bulk-pushes variable definitions

**Platform Token Sources:**
- **Web:** Tailwind CSS config (v3/v4) — colors, spacing, typography scales. Tools: [Tailwind Tokens Figma plugin](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/1513618945140968492), [Token Code plugin](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/1456356838028341739/token-code-css-tailwind-typescript-shopify-swift-android)
- **Android:** Material Design 3 tokens — [m3.material.io/foundations/design-tokens](https://m3.material.io/foundations/design-tokens/how-to-use-tokens). The Material Theme Builder Figma plugin already exports a Color.kt token set; JSON export drives Jetpack Compose theming.
- **iOS:** Apple Human Interface Guidelines color and typography tokens. Apple provides Figma templates at [developer.apple.com/design/resources](https://developer.apple.com/design/resources/). Tokens feed SwiftUI Dynamic Type.

**Style Dictionary:** The canonical token transformation library. Can take a Figma-exported JSON and transform it into CSS variables, Tailwind config, Swift constants, or Kotlin color files. Key pipeline: Figma Variables → JSON export → Style Dictionary transform → platform output.

**Feasibility:** High for web (Tailwind is well-mapped). Medium for Android/iOS (requires opinionated DL token schema and mapping to platform formats). Interactive setup wizard is achievable with the plugin's HTML UI panel.

**Open Questions / Risks:**
- ~~Does DL have an existing token schema?~~ ✅ Resolved — Tailwind primitives + platform collections, see Blocker 2
- Tailwind v4 uses CSS custom properties natively — token mapping changes from v3 config; skill should detect/support both
- Interactive wizard UX needs design: what questions to ask, in what order, with what defaults

---

### Skill 3: `/sync-design-system`

**Purpose:** Diff Figma variables/tokens against code tokens, then prompt the user to push changes to Figma or to code.

**Relevant APIs:**
- Figma Variables REST API `GET /v1/files/:file_key/variables/local` — reads all current Figma variables
- `figma.variables.getLocalVariableCollectionsAsync()` + `figma.variables.getLocalVariablesAsync()` — reads from within the plugin
- Figma Variables REST API `PUT /v1/files/:file_key/variables` — writes variable changes back to Figma
- Code-side: reads a `tokens.json` or Tailwind config from the local filesystem (requires plugin to call an external service or desktop companion — **important constraint**)

**Key Tool:** Style Dictionary can generate a normalized JSON from code tokens. The diff can be performed by comparing this JSON to the Figma Variables API response.

**Existing Reference:** [Nate Baldwin's Figma Variables sync approach](https://medium.com/@NateBaldwin/synchronizing-figma-variables-with-design-tokens-3a6c6adbf7da) — demonstrates bidirectional sync using the Variables REST API.

**Feasibility:** Medium. Reading Figma variables is straightforward. The challenge is reading code-side tokens: Figma plugins run in a sandboxed iframe and cannot access the local filesystem. Two architectural options:
1. **Plugin + companion CLI script** — a local Node.js script (run via CLI or VS Code task) reads tokens from code, calls the Figma REST API to compare and push
2. **Plugin reads from a GitHub URL** — user provides a URL to their `tokens.json` in GitHub; plugin fetches and diffs

Option 1 is more robust and aligns with how Figma Code Connect works.

**Open Questions / Risks:**
- Where do DL projects store their design tokens in code? (e.g., `tailwind.config.js`, `tokens.json`, CSS custom properties)
- Bidirectional sync (push to code) requires writing files to the local filesystem — needs CLI companion
- Conflict resolution UX: what if both Figma and code have changed the same token?

---

### Skill 4: `/create-component [component types]`

**Purpose:** Pull in shadcn/ui components, build them out on the Figma canvas, and optionally link them to Code Connect.

**Relevant APIs:**
- `figma.createComponent()`, `figma.createComponentSet()` — creates component and variant sets on canvas
- `figma.variables.*` — binds design tokens to component properties
- Figma Code Connect CLI (`@figma/code-connect`) — links Figma components to codebase component files

**shadcn/ui Integration:**
- [shadcn/ui has an official Figma kit](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/figma) with 40+ components
- [shadcndesign.com Code Connect guide](https://www.shadcndesign.com/blog/figma-code-connect-shadcn-ui-kit) demonstrates connecting the Figma kit to a codebase using Code Connect
- The "Figma to shadcn/ui" plugin (community) converts Figma designs to shadcn component code, but the inverse (code → canvas) is less automated

**Feasibility:** Medium-High. Building shadcn components on the Figma canvas programmatically is achievable but complex — each component requires correct variant structure, token bindings, and naming conventions that match the Code Connect mapping. The most practical approach:
1. Import the official shadcn/ui Figma community kit programmatically (or prompt user to import it)
2. Use the plugin to place, rename, and token-bind components
3. Run Code Connect CLI to publish mappings after placement

**Open Questions / Risks:**
- Figma community files cannot be "imported" programmatically — user must duplicate the kit manually
- Component creation is per-component — an initial list of supported component types is needed
- Code Connect property mapping (e.g., mapping `variant` prop to Figma variant) requires codebase-aware configuration

---

### Skill 5: `/code-connect`

**Purpose:** Run the Code Connect CLI to find Figma components that are missing code-connect mappings and prompt the agent to create them.

**Relevant APIs / Tools:**
- `@figma/code-connect` npm package — the official CLI
- `npx figma connect` — parses the codebase, finds components, suggests mappings
- `figma connect publish --token=<PAT>` — publishes mappings to Figma
- Figma MCP Server (`mcp__claude_ai_Figma__get_code_connect_suggestions`, `mcp__claude_ai_Figma__send_code_connect_mappings`) — these MCP tools can find suggestions and push mappings from within the agent environment
- `mcp__claude_ai_Figma__get_context_for_code_connect` — reads component context for mapping generation

**Feasibility:** High. The Figma MCP server already exposes `get_code_connect_suggestions` and `send_code_connect_mappings`, making this the most MCP-native skill of the seven. The workflow is:
1. Agent calls `get_code_connect_suggestions` on a Figma file
2. Agent reviews unmapped components
3. Agent generates Code Connect files using `get_context_for_code_connect`
4. Agent publishes via `send_code_connect_mappings` or CLI

**Open Questions / Risks:**
- Requires a Figma Personal Access Token (PAT) with `code_connect:write` scope — how is this managed per-user?
- CLI invocation from within the plugin requires a companion script or VS Code task (plugins cannot run shell commands)
- If using the MCP approach (agent context), the agent needs access to the codebase file tree

---

### Skill 6: `/new-language [language type]`

**Purpose:** Duplicate a frame/file in a new language for localization/translation.

**Relevant APIs:**
- `figma.currentPage.selection` — identifies selected frame
- `node.clone()` — duplicates the selected frame
- `figma.createPage()` — creates a new page for the language variant
- Plugin UI — language selector input
- Text node traversal: iterate `figma.currentPage.findAll(node => node.type === "TEXT")` to collect translatable strings

**Existing Ecosystem:**
- [Lokalise Figma plugin](https://lokalise.com/product/apps/design-tools/figma/) — links translation keys and can deliver localized frames per language
- [Crowdin Figma plugin](https://store.crowdin.com/figma) — pulls translations back into Figma, one page per language
- [CopyDoc](https://www.hypermatic.com/articles/copydoc-figma-localization-plugin/) — duplicates frames per locale from CSV/XLIFF/Google Sheets
- Custom approach: duplicate frame, traverse text nodes, call a translation API (e.g., DeepL, Google Translate) to populate translated strings

**Feasibility:** High for frame duplication and page creation. Medium if integrating a live translation API (requires API key management). Minimum viable version: duplicate the frame to a new page named for the language, extract all text nodes into a list for the designer to fill in. Full version: call a translation API automatically.

**Open Questions / Risks:**
- ~~Should the skill auto-translate or just scaffold?~~ ✅ Resolved — agent-driven inline translation, no external API
- ~~Does DL have an existing localization platform?~~ ✅ Resolved — no platform, agent handles translation
- Right-to-left (RTL) languages (Arabic, Hebrew) require layout mirroring — warn the designer, do not auto-flip
- Multi-file localization (e.g., shared component library) is out of scope for Phase 1

---

### Skill 7: `/accessibility-check`

**Purpose:** Check designs against WCAG AA guidelines and simulate Dynamic Type (iOS) and font scaling (Android) to show how designs react to larger text sizes.

**Relevant APIs:**
- Plugin API: `node.fills`, `node.strokes` — reads colors for contrast calculation
- Plugin API: text node properties (`fontSize`, `fontWeight`, `lineHeight`) — reads typography for size compliance
- WCAG contrast ratio formula (can be implemented in-plugin, no external API needed)
- Figma Simulation: `node.clone()` + text size manipulation simulates Dynamic Type scaling
- Plugin UI: renders accessibility report inline

**Existing Plugins for Reference:**
- [WCAG Plugin](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/1373362852131056921/wcag-plugin) — full WCAG 2.1 check
- [Corpowid WCAG Checker](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/1611140964405058830) — vision simulator + WCAG 2.1+
- [Text Contrast Checker (APCA WCAG3)](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/1383061356258316633) — APCA perceptual contrast
- [InspectRun](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/1463945065266231945) — contrast, text sizes, spacing

**Dynamic Type / Font Scaling Simulation:**
- iOS Dynamic Type has 12 accessibility sizes (xSmall → AX5). Apple provides the scaling table in the HIG.
- Android font scaling goes up to 200% (Android 14+). Google's Material 3 guidelines define the scale.
- Simulation approach: clone the selected frame, apply each font scale factor to all text nodes, render side-by-side for comparison.

**Feasibility:** High for WCAG contrast and text size checks (pure Plugin API). Medium for Dynamic Type simulation (requires programmatic text scaling and layout reflow — auto-layout frames will reflow; absolute-positioned text won't). Integrating a full WCAG report with pass/fail per element is well-precedented in the community plugin ecosystem.

**Open Questions / Risks:**
- Auto-layout vs. absolute positioning: simulation accuracy depends on frame structure
- WCAG 3.0 (APCA) vs. WCAG 2.1 — which standard should DL target? (Recommend WCAG 2.1 AA as baseline)
- Should the skill write a report to a new page/frame in Figma, or display inline in the plugin panel?
- iOS Liquid Glass design language (2025 HIG update) introduces new visual treatment that affects contrast checks

---

## Shared Infrastructure

The following infrastructure components are shared across multiple skills and should be built once:

| Component | Used By | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Variables API wrapper** | `/new-project`, `/create-design-system`, `/sync-design-system` | Read/write Figma variables via Plugin API and REST API |
| **Token JSON schema** | `/create-design-system`, `/sync-design-system` | DL canonical token format (color, typography, spacing, radius) |
| **Style Dictionary pipeline** | `/create-design-system`, `/sync-design-system` | Transforms tokens between Figma format and platform formats |
| **Code Connect helpers** | `/create-component`, `/code-connect` | PAT management, CLI invocation or MCP call wrappers |
| **Frame/node traversal utilities** | `/new-language`, `/accessibility-check` | Find all text nodes, clone frames, page creation |
| **Plugin UI shell** | All skills | Shared HTML panel, routing between skills, PAT/settings storage |

---

## Recommendations

1. **Build the Plugin UI shell first** — a shared panel with skill routing, settings storage (Figma `clientStorage`), and PAT management is a prerequisite for all skills.

2. **Define the DL token schema before building `/create-design-system` or `/sync-design-system`** — the schema drives everything downstream. Coordinate with designers to agree on naming conventions.

3. **Start `/code-connect` via the Figma MCP server** — it is the most readily achievable skill given the MCP tools available (`get_code_connect_suggestions`, `send_code_connect_mappings`). Use it as a proof-of-concept for the overall plugin→agent pipeline.

4. **`/accessibility-check` is a strong standalone deliverable** — it has no external dependencies, uses only the Plugin API, and delivers immediate value. Good candidate for an early sprint.

5. **`/new-language` should start with frame duplication only** — translation API integration is a Phase 2 enhancement. Confirm whether DL uses an existing localization platform before integrating one.

6. **`/sync-design-system` requires a CLI companion** — the plugin sandbox cannot read local files. Design a small Node.js CLI script that reads the codebase tokens and calls the Figma REST API. This companion script is also useful for CI/CD token sync pipelines.

7. **`/create-component` depends on shadcn kit import** — since Figma community files cannot be imported programmatically, document the manual prerequisite step: duplicate the official shadcn/ui Figma kit into the project before running the skill.

---

## Open Questions (Cross-Skill)

~~1. Does Detroit Labs have an existing design token schema?~~ ✅ Resolved — see Blocker 2
~~2. Which Figma plan does DL use?~~ ✅ Resolved — see Blocker 3 (non-blocking, REST path is an enhancement)
~~3. Is there an existing DL project template in Figma that `/new-project` should replicate?~~ ✅ Resolved — see Blocker 1
~~4. Does DL use any existing localization platforms?~~ ✅ Resolved — see Blocker 4 (agent-driven, no platform)

5. How are Figma Personal Access Tokens managed for designers? (PATs are needed for REST API calls and Code Connect publish — needs a UX decision: plugin prompts for PAT on first run and stores in `figma.clientStorage`, or team shares a service PAT)
6. Will the plugin be distributed via Figma Community, or only internally via the Organization?
7. Should skills that write to Figma canvas use a "dry run" preview mode before committing changes?
8. What are the exact page names / internal structure of the Foundations file? (Needs a live Figma session to inspect `HrSL0JGanWIAbcJOJAyucz` page structure — MCP could not access it without an active selection)
